entries.title: -Entering Your Name-
entries.content: Make sure to enter your last name first and your first name second. The canon name in the anime and video games is Yu Narukami, and the canon name for the manga is Souji Seta.

entries.title: -Responses-
entries.content: You frequently will be able to respond to a character by choosing a response to give. Normally during the story these responses don't matter. However, there are certain responses late in the game that determine what ending you will get. I will make sure to make note of them when they happen.

entries.title: -Exploration-
entries.content: You are given a chance to look around the immediate area next to the gas station. You can talk to NPCs to discover some lore, get advice or hints of what to do, and hints of other activities that may be available to you.

entries.title: IMPORTANT! IMPORTANT! IMPORTANT!
entries.content: There is also a blue butterfly to save your game. Make sure to save often! (WAQ1: Why do I need to save frequently?)

entries.title: -Fridge-
entries.content: If you check the fridge, you will find some Fried Rice. Feel free to eat it, but nothing will happen. There will be a new food item in the fridge every Monday. Some food items will greatly increase your Courage. which is a Status Parameter.

entries.title: IMPORTANT! IMPORTANT! IMPORTANT!
entries.content: If I don't mention a food item on Monday, don't look in the fridge! Sometimes we will save the food for a different day, because it may take time. So be mindful of when to check the fridge!

entries.content: Here you can sleep to pass time. If you sleep in the futon any day but today, you will dream about a Social Link and earn bonus points toward it. However, we will never be making use of this. The futon gets no love from us.

entries.content: At certain points in the game, time will be spent completing tasks related to the main story. When this happens, I will skip to when we next have control of how to spend time.

entries.title: -Advice-
entries.content: Outside of fulfilling certain objectives for 100% completion, this walkthrough will not focus on dungeons or battles. However, I will be offering advice as we go through the game to help you clear dungeons easily. To start, there are two basic types of attacks you can use. Melee attacks, and Persona Skills. Melee attacks are weaker than other attacks, but weapons can be bought later in the game to increase damage. Persona Skills are more powerful, but will cost either HP or SP. Keep a close eye on your health, because if MC's HP hits 0, then it's game over.

entries.title: -Advice-
entries.content:
Each Skill has a certain type of affinity, and most enemies are weak to certain affinities. Hitting an enemy's weakness, you will inflict Down status. When an enemy is knocked down, you will obtain a One More, and can take another turn. You can chain multiple One Mores together as long as you keep knocking enemies down. By hitting enemy's weaknesses and knocking down every enemy, you can perform an All-Out Attack. This is a hard hitting attack of Almighty damage to all enemies, and will usually kill them instantly. Using All-Out Attacks can be a saving grace in an otherwise difficult battle. This system is a crucial part of battles, and understanding this mechanic is important to deal with harder enemies later down the line.
MC has the power of the Wild Card, which means he can wield multiple Personas. Because of this, MC is the most versatile Party Member. However, keep in mind that weaknesses and stats change depending on which Persona is equipped. Enemies can and will punish you if you aren't paying attention to the Persona you have equipped.
You can now change Party Tactics. While the AI is decent, I highly recommend putting them on Direct Commands. It will save you from headaches later.

entries.title: -Shuffle Time-
entries.content: Shuffle Time is one of two ways to obtain Personas. Shuffle Time is obtained by exploiting enemy weaknesses, using All-Out Attacks, and finishing the battle quickly. If there are no Personas to collect, your priority should be something close to this: Sweep Bonus, Magician, Temperance, Hermit, Emperor. Getting a Sweep Bonus guarantees Shuffle Time next battle, gives you 3 picks instead of one, and gets rid of cards with negative effects.

entries.content: In the Shopping District South, you can buy soda from the vending machines. TaP Soda costs 120 yen, and it restores 10 SP, which is hard to come by. You can buy 5 a week, and it restocks on Monday. I would recommend investing in these, especially if you find yourself having trouble with saving SP.

entries.title: -Advice-
entries.content:
From here on out, we need to finish every dungeon in one day. This can be tricky, especially in the early game. For this dungeon, we will use 3 Ball Lightnings to take out one Golden Shadow. (WAQ7: How do you beat a dungeon in one day?)
You can now fuse Personas. You may notice that MC's Personas level up slower compared to the rest of the team. In order to stay caught up with the team, you will need to fuse Personas constantly. You will also need to fuse certain Personas for Social Links. More on that later.
Because of how expensive things are in this game, we will need to get as much money as possible. The best ways to do this is to grind from Golden Shadows. We will need at least 50,000 Yen before we leave.

entries.title: -The Reaper-
entries.content: After opening 20 chests, a random chest on the floor you are on will contain The Reaper. He is a Lv 85 optional boss. I will note when I decide to take him down.

entries.content:
HIGHLY IMPORTANT! HIGHLY IMPORTANT! HIGHLY IMPORTANT! HIGHLY IMPORTANT! HIGHLY IMPORTANT!
There is a dialog tree that will determine the ending you get. There are 5 Possible Endings for Persona 4, and 7 Possible Endings for Persona 4 Golden. I will map out the options for each ending, but to avoid major spoilers I will not map out what each dialog option says. If you want to try get the Good Ending by yourself, then don't look ahead until you finish today. However, it can be difficult to get it first try. You should expect minor spoilers from here on out, but I will be doing my best to take out as much story related spoilers as possible. You have been warned.

entries.title: Exams
entries.content:
Question: The word "alphabet" comes from the words "alpha" and what other one? Option B: Beta. Question: How was the theory that the pyramids were built by slaves disproven? Option A: Attendance logs.
Question: What drink name means "bury demons"? Option D: Toso. Question: What is the medical term for brainfreeze? Option C: Sphenopalatine ganglioneuralgia.
Question: What is the Japanese zodiac equivalent to the "cat" in the Thai and Vietnamese zodiacs? Option B: Rabbit. What vegetable was used to make the first jack-o'-lanterns? Option A: Turnips.
Question: What color were the pyramids when they were first built? Option B: White. Question: What gets mixed with snow in Europe that sometimes causes it to turn red? Option B: The Sahara Desert sand.

blocks.title: New Game Plus Walkthrough
content_blocks: This checklist is for the New Game Plus playthrough to complete the game. There will be a complete walkthrough added in a later version of the guide. Required tasks are needed for the Golden Completed Trophy. Optional tasks are extra content you can view in a second playthrough, but isn't required for the Golden Competed Trophy.
Complete the Compendium Beat the Secret Boss Obtain Big Bro is Worried Trophy (Visit Nanako in the hospital 3 times) Obtain Hardcore Risette Fan Trophy (Hear 250 of Rise's navigation lines)
